commentaries.author: Erasmus of Rotterdam
commentaries.content: He had been born lame, and he had displayed his debility for so many years as a beggar that no one could say in slander that this deformity was simulated or insignificant.

commentaries.author: Interlinear Gloss
commentaries.content: having verified that they were men without letters or learning BEDE They were called 'without letters' who were content with only their own language and natural knowledge, and did not know the study of letters.

commentaries.author: Irenaeus of Lyons
commentaries.content: They were confounded, therefore, both by this instance of healing ("for the man was above forty years old on whom this miracle of healing took place"
